checks.a_analysis: The output document contains the main heading, date, legend titles, legend items, and source information exactly as they appear in the source image. The metadata title 'Map Imagery Logo Imagery 4809' is an expected AI-generated addition for accessibility and not a fabrication of document content. There is no invented body text or section headings that do not have a basis in the source.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, URLs) in the source document, and none has been added to the output.

All sections of the single-page source document are accounted for in the output. This includes the primary headers, the complete legend list, the source citations, the map (provided as an image), and the organization logo (provided as an image).
The source document does not contain any tables. The list of parcel updates is correctly formatted as a list in the output, containing all 10 entries found in the source legend.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content within the defined sections (Headers, Legend, Sources) is fully captured without omission. While individual county labels within the map graphic are not extracted as separate text nodes, they are preserved within the image element itself, which is consistent with standard document structure conversion.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the source exactly. All dates, numbers in parentheses in the legend, and organization names in the sources section are correct.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source document and the converted output. The text from the title, date, legend ('Parcel Updates'), and sources section appears only once in the output, consistent with the source document. No duplicate text blocks were found.

The heading hierarchy in the output document contains a skip. It begins with an H1 for the title and H2 for the date, but then proceeds to an H4 for 'Parcel Updates:'. According to the audit criteria, skipping a level (from H2 to H4 without an intervening H3) is a structural defect as it affects screen reader navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document contains headings for the title 'NC Parcels Project Status by County' and the section 'Parcel Updates:'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections[0], elements at index 1 and 2 (reading_order 2 and 3)

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables. It consists of a map, a legend (which was correctly converted to a list), and text blocks. The output does not attempt to create any tables.

The reading order in the output flows logically from top to bottom: Title, Date, Legend, Sources, followed by the Map and Logo images. While the legend and sources are physically located at the bottom-left of the original map, placing them before the image in the structured output is an acceptable reordering for accessibility and does not disrupt the meaning of the content.

The legend under 'Parcel Updates:' has been converted into an unordered list. This accurately represents the structural relationship of the legend items in the source document. The content of each list item matches the text in the source legend.
The source document contains no URLs or contact links. No links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML document follows a logical linear flow that mirrors the hierarchy of the source PDF. The title and date are at the top, followed by the legend and source information. While the map image is placed at the end of the content (represented by alt text) and the layout has shifted from a graphical map-centric design to a text-based list, the logical sequence is maintained. The addition of a Table of Contents is an expected accessibility feature and does not disrupt the visual flow.

The source document does not contain any formal data tables. The legend and list of sources are formatted as text blocks or lists in both the source and the output. No visual corruption of table-like structures is observed.

Multiple meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the rendered HTML output. The primary visual content, the color-coded map of North Carolina counties, is absent and only represented by its alternative text description. The NCGICC logo is also missing. Additionally, the color-coded squares in the legend ('Parcel Updates') are missing, which are critical for visually interpreting the data presented in the source map.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF features a prominent color-coded map of North Carolina (center), an NCGICC logo (bottom right), and 10 color-coded square swatches in the 'Parcel Updates' legend (bottom left).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the map and logo are missing, replaced by text descriptions ('A map of North Carolina...', 'NCGICC logo'). The 'Parcel Updates' legend is rendered as a plain bulleted list without the color swatches.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main document body, following the 'Sources' section and within the 'Parcel Updates' list.

checks.a_analysis: No genuine body content duplication is observed. The repetition of document titles and section headings ('NC Parcels Project Status by County', 'June 25, 2025', 'Parcel Updates:') in both the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body is an intentional and expected accessibility feature for document navigation.
